data.title: SoFi Stock Is On The Move: Critical Levels To Watch
data.description:
• SoFi Technologies stock is building positive momentum. What’s driving SOFI shares up?

The Tape Is Friendly, But The Stock Still Has Work To Do

SoFi is sitting in the middle of its 52-week range, with a $10.41 low and a $32.73 high — an awkward but familiar spot for a former momentum darling trying to rebuild credibility after a run-up and pullback. The short-term picture is undeniably perky: the stock is trading 15.9% above its 20-day simple moving average. The intermediate-term trend, however, is still playing hard to get, with shares 14.7% below the 100-day SMA.

The 20-day SMA remains below the 50-day SMA, and the death cross in March (50-day SMA below the 200-day SMA) keeps the longer trend biased cautious until price can reclaim more long-term levels. Even so, the MACD is above its signal line with a positive histogram, which leans toward improving upside pressure versus the prior downswing.

The longer-term tape has been generous. SoFi is up 79.60% over the last 12 months. Now, the next debate is whether that strength can translate into a clean breakout rather than another rally that runs into supply. The stock is still in "repair mode," where sentiment can do as much heavy lifting as fundamentals.

SoFi's Real Fight Is Over $22.50

The market has already drawn the map. The $22.50 area has acted as a ceiling where rallies have recently stalled and sellers have shown up, making it the level that separates "bounce" from "trend." On the downside, $16.50 has been the level where demand tends to appear on pullbacks.

If the stock can't chew through resistance, the risk is that the current move becomes another short-term pop inside a longer-term consolidation. If it can, the narrative shifts quickly from "repair" to "re-rating," and that's where the next catalyst comes in.

What Are Analysts Saying?

Analysts aren't exactly pounding the table. The stock carries a Hold Rating with an average price target of $25.25, and recent moves show a cautious tone:

  • Argus Research: Initiated with Hold (April 15)
  • Goldman Sachs: Neutral (Lowers Target to $20.00) (April 14)
  • Wells Fargo: Equal-Weight (Lowers Target to $18.00) (April 9)

That's not a bearish chorus, but it's also not the kind of enthusiasm that powers sustained multiple expansion, which means earnings don’t just need to be "good," they need to be clarifying.

Factor Signals: Growth Is Strong, Momentum Is Not A Slam Dunk

On the Benzinga Edge scorecard, SoFi screens as growth-heavy, but the market isn't treating the current move as a runaway train.

  • Momentum is Neutral (Score: 38.56)
  • Growth is Strong (Score: 92.33).

The profile can support premium multiples, but the stock is still sensitive to catalysts and overhead resistance because the trend isn't universally convincing yet. For traders who want deeper screening tools and real-time signals around those factor shifts, platforms like premium market tools can help contextualize the move.

That sensitivity is exactly why the next few weeks matter. A growth-heavy stock with only moderate momentum can rally hard on confirmation, or roll over quickly if the story wobbles.

SOFI Stock On The Move

SOFI Price Action: SoFi shares were up 1.12% at $19.01 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: Why Is Avis Budget Group Stock Surging Thursday?
data.description:
The Nasdaq is up 0.51% while the S&P 500 has gained 0.25%.

• Avis Budget Group stock is approaching key resistance levels. Why are CAR shares at highs?

Circuit Breakers Triggered Amid Rally

On Thursday, Avis Budget Group shares were halted after triggering an upside circuit breaker. At the time of the halt, the stock gained 7.49%. Trading later resumed as buyers continued to flood the tape.

Avis Budget Group provides mobility solutions through three core brands: Avis, Budget and Zipcar.

Short Interest Reaches Critical Levels

Short interest recently climbed from 8.12 million to 9.03 million shares. Data shows that 53.66% of the company’s publicly available shares are currently held short.

Based on the average daily volume of 1.24 million shares, shorts would need 7.27 days to exit their positions.

Technical Analysis

Avis Budget is sitting under its 52-week high of $448. The stock is trading 98.9% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 197.6% above its 100-day SMA.

The relative strength index (RSI), a momentum gauge, is 89.31, which points to overheated momentum and a higher risk of sharp pullbacks.

  • Key Resistance: $415.50
  • Key Support: $204

CAR Stock Price Activity: Avis Budget Group shares were up 7.43% at $425.19 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo: Ian Dewar Photography / Shutterstock.com

data.title: Why Is GameStop Stock Up Today?
data.description:
• GameStop shares are advancing steadily. Why are GME shares climbing?

Power Packs Launch Opens A New Revenue Angle

GameStop's latest catalyst is the public launch of "Power Packs," a digital trading card platform that lets collectors buy digital packs that can unlock physical PSA-graded cards, including Pokémon and major sports categories.

Packs are priced from $25 to $2,500, and investors are framing it as a higher-margin collectibles expansion with potential recurring digital transaction revenue.

Short-interest data is also part of the setup, with short interest recently falling to 62.82 million shares from 64.23 million, or 15.34% of float, and about 10.36 days to cover at current volumes. That short-cover math matters for GME because a thinner short base can still create sharp squeezes when days-to-cover sits above 10 and volume spikes.

Traders Shrug Off Steve Eisman's Criticism

Technical Setup Leans Bullish Despite Mixed Signals

GameStop is sitting in the middle of its 52-week range ($19.93 to $35.81), which indicates the stock is still trading within a wide, two-sided band rather than trending at an extreme. It's trading 9.2% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 10.6% above its 100-day SMA (at $25.44), a setup that leans toward buyers controlling the short- and intermediate-term tape.

The moving average backdrop is mixed: the 20-day SMA remains below the 50-day SMA (a bearish short-term crossover), but the golden cross in March (50-day SMA above the 200-day SMA) keeps the longer trend from looking broken. That combination often shows up when a stock is rebounding but still needs time to "repair" the shorter-term structure.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, has the MACD line above the signal line with a positive histogram, which points to improving upside momentum versus the prior downswing. In everyday terms, MACD is saying the recent push higher has more force than the recent pullbacks, even if it can still chop around.

  • Key Resistance: $28.00 — a level where rallies have recently stalled and sellers may reappear.
  • Key Support: $22.50 — an area where buyers have tended to step in and defend pullbacks.

GME Shares Edge Higher Thursday Afternoon

GME Stock Price Activity: GameStop shares were up 1.98% at $25.28 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: What's Going On With AT&T Stock Thursday?
data.description:
AT&T Inc. (NYSE:T) shares are trading higher on Thursday as risk appetite stays constructive, with Communication Services leading alongside other top-performing groups.

The Nasdaq is up 0.70% while the S&P 500 has gained 0.35%, and the Communication Services sector is adding about 1.2%, giving T a supportive tape to bounce with.

This month, AT&T rolled out a series of major deals and product launches aimed at strengthening its network leadership and customer experience.

$2 Billion Push To Power Next-Gen Emergency Network

Under the deal, AT&T will cut FirstNet costs by roughly $1 billion—freeing up capital to accelerate investment in the Nationwide Public Safety Broadband Network and fast-track deployment of a dedicated public safety 5G core.

OneConnect Launch Targets Simplicity And Retention

AT&T also introduced OneConnect, a bundled subscription designed to simplify how customers manage connectivity.

The new offering combines wireless and home internet into a single monthly plan, eliminating multiple bills and streamlining account management.

The move directly targets rising demand for convenience, with 72% of users preferring a single, unified bill—positioning AT&T to boost both customer satisfaction and retention.

What Is Driving AT&T’s Stock Rally Today?

Thursday's push higher fits a "relief rally" feel after the stock's momentum got stretched to the downside, with traders reacting to oversold signals and nearby longer-term trend lines. With market breadth positive (advancers leading by about 1.8 to 1), dip-buying is showing up more readily in large, defensive-leaning names.

Communication Services is also one of the day's leaders, ranking third out of 11 sectors, which can amplify moves in bellwether telecom and media stocks. That sector bid matters because it tends to pull capital toward higher-yielding, cash-flow stories when the broader market is green.

The broader backdrop is broadly "risk-on," with the Nasdaq up 0.70% and the S&P 500 up 0.35%, while seven sectors are advancing versus four declining. Energy (+1.48%) and Technology (+1.19%) are also strong, reinforcing the idea that buyers are leaning into equities rather than hiding out.

Critical Levels To Watch for AT&T Stock

AT&T is sitting in the middle of its 52-week range ($22.95 to $29.79), which points to a market still debating direction after earlier swings. The stock is trading 5.5% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 0.1% above its 100-day SMA, a mix that leans weak short-term but closer to "holding ground" on the intermediate trend near $26.28.

The relative strength index (RSI), a momentum gauge, is 26.56, which is firmly oversold and often lines up with selling pressure getting crowded. RSI at 26.56 tells you sellers have been in control lately, even as rebounds can start to appear.

  • Key Resistance: $29.50 — a level where rallies have recently struggled to keep pushing higher.
  • Key Support: $23.50 — an area where buyers have tended to step in and defend pullbacks.

Over the past 12 months, the stock is down 2.78%, which is consistent with a choppy, range-bound tape rather than a clean uptrend. The golden cross in March (50-day SMA above the 200-day SMA) still argues the longer-term structure isn't broken, but the stock being below the 200-day SMA keeps that bullish signal under pressure.

How AT&T Compares to Communication Services Peers

AT&T is outperforming its Communication Services peers today, up 3.22% versus the sector's 1.18% gain, a lead of about 2.04 percentage points. With Communication Services ranking 3 out of 11 sectors, the group tailwind is real, but T is doing more than just "riding beta."

Zooming out, the sector is up 2.92% over the past 30 days and up 3.10% over the past 90 days, which supports the idea that money has been rotating back into the space. If the sector stays near the top of the leaderboard, relative strength like today's can matter because it often draws incremental flows toward the leaders.

AT&T Earnings Preview: What Analysts Expect

The countdown is on: AT&T is set to report earnings on April 22, 2026 (confirmed).

  • EPS Estimate: 55 cents (Up from 51 cents YoY)
  • Revenue Estimate: $31.22 Billion (Up from $30.63 Billion YoY)
  • Valuation: P/E of 8.4x (Indicates value opportunity relative to peers)

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $30.40. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Scotiabank: Sector Perform (Raises Target to $31.50) (April 1)
  • Keybanc: Overweight (Raises Target to $36.00) (March 25)
  • Citigroup: Buy (Raises Target to $31.50) (March 23)

AT&T Benzinga Edge Rankings: Growth and Value Insights

Below is the Benzinga Edge scorecard for AT&T, highlighting its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market:

  • Quality: Neutral (Score: 55.21) — The fundamentals read as steady, not standout, versus the broader market.
  • Value: Neutral (Score: 41.7) — Valuation looks middle-of-the-pack on this model, not a deep bargain signal.
  • Growth: Strong (Score: 83.07) — The scorecard flags better growth characteristics than many peers.

The Verdict: AT&T’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a growth-tilted profile with neutral value and quality readings. With momentum not scored here, the chart setup (oversold RSI and mixed moving averages) becomes the key swing factor traders are watching into earnings.

Top ETFs Holding AT&T: XLC, IXP, and GQGU

Significance: Because T carries such a heavy weight in these funds, any significant inflows or outflows for these ETFs will likely force automatic buying or selling of the stock.

AT&T Stock Price Movement on Thursday

T Stock Price Activity: AT&T shares were up 3.22% at $26.28 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Photo via Shutterstock

data.title: What's Behind The Jump In Datavault Stock?
data.description:
• Datavault AI stock is surging to new heights today. Why is DVLT stock surging?

GPU Network Sites Come Online

Datavault AI Inc., which offers a cloud-based platform that can be used to create digital twins, just announced that its 48,000-GPU fleet has come online. The GPU network will help the company with quantum-ready, high-performance computing that will be accessible from 30 cities by the middle of the year.

The network is worth between $1.44 billion and $1.92 billion, and is scheduled to be revenue-generating by the end of the year. The fleet is seen as an alternative source of secure enterprise AI compute capacity in a market where hyperscale cloud providers are in high demand.

“The GPU supply crisis has created a two-tier market – hyperscalers with capacity and enterprises waiting in a year-long queue. Our quantum-ready fleet, built on SanQtum AI’s cyber-secure edge architecture, gives enterprises a path to secure AI compute, data scoring, and tokenized monetization without waiting for hyperscaler allocations,” said Nathaniel T. Bradley, the CEO of Datavault AI Inc.

Shares Trades Between Key Averages

Datavault AI is sitting in the lower half of its 52-week range (from 25 cents to $4.10), which keeps the longer-term recovery story tentative despite the bounce. The stock is trading 15% above its 20-day simple moving average (SMA) and 17.6% below its 100-day SMA, a split that suggests short-term momentum is improving while the intermediate trend still needs repair.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, has the MACD line above the signal line with a positive histogram, which leans toward improving upside pressure versus the prior downtrend. That "above the signal line" setup is the chart's way of saying buyers have been gaining traction more recently than sellers.

The death cross in February (50-day SMA below the 200-day SMA) still hangs over the longer-term picture, which is consistent with why rallies can run into overhead supply. Over the last 12 months, the stock is up 1.98%, which points to a choppy, range-like tape rather than a clean sustained trend.

  • Key Resistance: $1.00 — a round-number area where rallies have recently stalled
  • Key Support: 50 cents — a nearby "line in the sand" area traders often defend after pullbacks

Shares Rise On Thursday

DVLT Stock Price Activity: Datavault AI shares were up 11.43% at $0.78 at the time of publication on Thursday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image: Shutterstock
